world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Random encounters heavily feature Nobunaga's troops; NPCs express desperation/rebellion themes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Province map dynamically updates showing shifting territorial control based on player-influenced battles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC behavior changes based on lord loyalty; recruitment pools shift; quest access gated by regional control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer independent daimyo; map consolidates showing reduced color variety; Nobunaga territory still dominant unless player intervenes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dungeon enemies escalate; final confrontation approaches; NPC urgency increases

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Game over condition (1601 without Nobunaga defeated)

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No continued progression; state forces terminal event

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Encounter tables differ; random battle spawn pools determined by path selection; visual enemy design shifts themes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Province ownership transfer; visual province color change indicating player-induced political shift
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Daimyo trust and loyalty status updated; recruitment availability changes; battle availability gated by regional control

environmental_storytelling.example: Iga clan's ruined settlement establishes personal stakes and narrative motivation; empty structures communicate past catastrophe
Territorial coloration shows Nobunaga's expansionist threat; visual dominance communicates urgency and scale of antagonist's power

Ninja, sage, warrior, samurai, ronin, hermit classes visually signal faction affiliation, profession, and recruitment availability; rival ninja classes (Koga vs. Iga) suggest faction conflict through roster distinction
18 dungeons block player advancement; hazard level and enemy encounters signal narrative progression and world danger escalation
Safe interior environments signal respite; NPC presence indicates recruitment opportunity; spatial distinction from combat zones communicates diplomatic sphere
Monster type variance by region/path signals world-state (magician path Western creatures suggest alternate magical narrative branch); outlaw presence indicates lawlessness
Date serves as implicit narrative pressure; player must visually see Nobunaga's expansion accelerating toward victory, communicating time scarcity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Alone; solitary dungeon navigation; dependent on NPC recruitment for survival
Leads coalition; multiple companions in combat grid; proactive dungeon conquest; initiates provincial battles
Unavailable for recruitment; refuses party join; appears as neutral or distant in dialogue
Joins party; participates in turn-based combat; provides magical/combat support; loyal NPC indicator
Cannot be reliably recruited; likely appears as hostile encounter or refuses repeated overtures; loyalty resistance visual/mechanical
Province expansion continues; army encounters increase; threatens multiple regions; temporal pressure escalates
Player-controlled allied daimyo begin encroaching on Nobunaga territory; final dungeon becomes accessible; endgame encounter initiated
Province ownership stable; recruitment available if player builds trust via espionage/sabotage
Province color shifts to player-friendly; battle support available; continued territorial expansion possible; recruitment pool expands
Turn-based grid combat initiation; attackable; lootable; normal difficulty scaling
Path-dependent spawn; suggests higher magical threat tier; distinct visual/behavioral movesets inferred

notes: Inindo uses a feudal Japan warring states setting with dual narrative systems (normal/magician path) that diverge enemy encounter visuals post-dungeon-1. The game communicates world-state primarily through province map coloration (Nobunaga's expansion threat), NPC recruitment availability (trust-gated), and temporal pressure (1601 game-over deadline). Faction identity is expressed through class rosters (Iga vs. Koga ninja distinction) rather than explicit color palettes or sigils (source lacks visual detail). Danger is signaled via random encounter initiation, enemy pool variance by path, and territorial map showing antagonist dominance. Safety manifests in social safe zones (inns/tea houses) and party-building mechanics. The 18-dungeon progression system gates narrative advancement and world-state changes. Rival clan mechanics (Koga unavailable for recruitment) enforce faction loyalty through mechanical resistance. Environmental storytelling relies on destroyed-village origin, province control visualization, and class-based NPC identity rather than detailed environmental props.
